text.title: django-quality-queries Failure Modes
text.summary: Five antipatterns with concrete symptom, root cause, and fix. Each maps to a rule from `01-core-rules.xml` so the validator can flag it.

antipattern.description: Adding `select_related("nullable_fk")` and then assuming the related object is never None.
antipattern.symptom: AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'email' in production list views; the same code works in tests.
antipattern.root-cause: select_related on a nullable FK uses an OUTER JOIN and correctly returns None for missing rows. The optimisation is sound; the access pattern is not.
antipattern.fix: Either guard with `if obj.user is not None:` or chain `.exclude(user__isnull=True)` to narrow the queryset semantics intentionally. Never remove select_related to "fix" the None handling.

antipattern.description: Calling `prefetch_related("items")` then filtering `obj.items.filter(active=True)` inside the loop.
antipattern.symptom: nplusone middleware fires; Debug Toolbar shows 2 + N queries despite the prefetch.
antipattern.root-cause: `obj.items.filter(...)` re-issues a SQL query because the filter constructs a new queryset that bypasses the prefetch cache.
antipattern.fix: Use `Prefetch("items", queryset=Item.objects.filter(active=True), to_attr="active_items")` and iterate `obj.active_items` (a plain list).

antipattern.description: Adding `select_related(...)` to every queryset including ones that never access the related object.
antipattern.symptom: Query time on detail endpoints rises 10-30%; database CPU climbs; EXPLAIN ANALYZE shows unnecessary JOINs.
antipattern.root-cause: Each JOIN costs CPU + memory; blanket optimisation defeats the point of per-endpoint tuning.
antipattern.fix: Profile with Debug Toolbar first. Add select_related only where the related field is actually dereferenced. Remove blanket calls from base managers.

antipattern.description: Calling `prefetch_related` after the queryset has been materialised (`list(qs)`).
antipattern.symptom: Optimisation appears to be in place but Debug Toolbar still shows N+1; tests fail under nplusone.
antipattern.root-cause: prefetch_related works at queryset evaluation time. Once the queryset is consumed (list, len, iteration, indexing), the prefetch call is a no-op.
antipattern.fix: Place `prefetch_related` before any materialisation. For already-materialised lists, call `prefetch_related_objects(items, "relation")` explicitly.

antipattern.description: Iterating model instances and calling `.save()` for counter increments or batch creates.
antipattern.symptom: Single endpoint executes thousands of UPDATEs; transaction time blows past 30s; database connection pool exhausted.
antipattern.root-cause: Each `.save()` is its own UPDATE round trip; counter increments also expose a race window between read and write.
antipattern.fix: Use `Model.objects.bulk_create(...)` for batch creates, `Model.objects.filter(pk__in=...).update(field=F("field") + 1)` for counters. Atomic + O(1) queries.
